calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Modify The Trust Terms
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: James Mitchell for and with Claude Perasso (Petitioner).

Issues with regard to the equal division of the trust into two and excerising a limited powers of appointment fbo their surviving spouse were addressed. The Court is not sure if the trust can be modified to provide for a different beneficiary. Mr. Mitchell argued that all parties have consented. The Court stated it is obligated to see that the Testator's intent is carry out and that the Petition is partially granted to equal division of the trust into two. Mr. Mitchell will prepare an order to include the exact language on the division of the trust.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Sr. Deputy M. Benton.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Modify The Trust Terms
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Confirming That Real Property Is Trust Property And Petition For Order Modifying
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Mitchell for Claude Perasso.

The Court stated that there was no problem with regard to the real property but that it did have concerns with regard to modifying the Trust terms. The Court and counsel confer regarding whether or not what is being requested is a material change to the trust. Mr. Mitchell stated that he did not want to trip over any adverse tax issues. The Court stated that it appears to be a material change to the trust, that for the last 30 years the income that flows from the trust is split in equal shares to Judge Perasso and his sister. Mr. Mitchell stated that even though it is a significant modification the families have agreed. The Court stated that it could be addressed by gifting. Mr. Mitchell stated that gifting has caps and may create tax consequences, that the current beneficiaries are in their eighties, that everyone is in agreement, that there are consents with regard to those that have an interest in the trust, and that they looked into alternatives but they present a host of problems. The Court again stated that it believed that it is a material change to the trust and that it was not inclined to grant the trust modification as currently drafted. Mr. Mitchell proposed that he confirm the division of the trust at 50/50, work in an income limitation, and clarify terms that will state that no change will occur until after the death of the two beneficiaries. The Court stated that it is willing to continue the petition to allow counsel time to file an amended petition, that it was bifurcating the Petition re: confirming that the Real Property is Trust Property and Granting that part (subject to review of the written order). The Petition to Modify the Trust terms is continued to January 16, 2013. The Court noted that it would appear that the grandchildren would need legal advice regarding this issue. Mr. Mitchell stated that he believed that the grandchildren and Judge Perasso's Sister were advised with regard to this issue.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.

Petition For Order Confirming That Real Property Is Trust Property And Petition For Order Modifying
Off Calendar Nov-14-2012 Continued To Nov-28-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
